mnemonics.title: CPP - Class Public Private Protected
mnemonics.content: CPP: Class members are Private by default, Public needs keyword, Protected for inheritance. Remember: 'Private Comes Preconfigured' - Private is default!

mnemonics.title: Constructor Sequence
mnemonics.content: Constructors Run Automatically When Creating Objects. CRADLE: Constructor Initializes, Destructor Cleans, Remember Objects Get Space When Born, Memory Released When Dying.

mnemonics.title: Static Members
mnemonics.content: STATIC: Shared Among All, Type Belongs to Class, Accessible Without Object, Not in This Pointer, Lifetime is Program Duration.

mnemonics.title: Access Specifiers Priority
mnemonics.content: Remember order in class: Private first by default! P-P-P: Private is Primary, Protected for Progeny, Public for Everybody.

acronyms.usage: Programming paradigm based on objects and classes
Focuses on data encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, abstraction
Visual representation of classes and relationships
Special constructor to create copy of another object
Compiler optimization that may skip copy constructor calls

visualTips.visualization: Think of a class as a blueprint/cookie cutter - it's the template. Objects are the cookies made from that blueprint. The blueprint defines what each cookie will have (properties) and what it can do (methods).
Visualize a house: Public = front door (anyone can enter lobby), Private = bedroom (only owner), Protected = inherited room (children can use).
Each object has its own copy of non-static data. The 'this' pointer is like your name tag - it tells functions which object they are working on right now.
Static members are like a shared notebook in a classroom - one copy that everyone (all objects) can read and write to.
Constructor is like the birth certificate (starts when you exist), Destructor is like the death certificate (cleans up your affairs when you're gone).

analogies.analogy: Class is like a recipe card, Object is like the actual cake made from that recipe. One recipe can make many cakes.
Private members are like your bank balance - the class controls access to prevent unauthorized changes, just like only you should access your bank account.
A friend function is like a family friend who, despite not living in your house (class), is given a spare key to access your private rooms when needed.
A const member function is like a 'read-only' mode - you can look at the object but cannot change its contents, like reading a book.
Static data is like a community fridge - one shared resource that all objects (residents) can access, not owned by any single person.
